Package nonprofitbookkeeping.ui.actions.scaledger

Class XlsmTableViewerFX

java.lang.Object
nonprofitbookkeeping.ui.actions.scaledger.XlsmTableViewerFX
public class XlsmTableViewerFX extends Object
Utility class for reading data from an Excel XLSM file (macro-enabled workbook) and converting a specified sheet into a Swing DefaultTableModel.

This class is designed to be used in a JavaFX environment where the DefaultTableModel might serve as an intermediate data structure before being potentially converted or adapted for use with JavaFX UI components like TableView. It uses Apache POI for handling Excel file operations.

  • Method Details

    • readXlsmToTableModel

      public static DefaultTableModel readXlsmToTableModel(File file, int sheetIndex) throws Exception
      Reads a specified sheet from an Excel (XLSM) file and converts its content into a Swing DefaultTableModel.

      The method assumes the following structure for the Excel sheet:

      • The first row of the sheet (obtained by sheet.getFirstRowNum()) is treated as the header row, and its cell values are used as column identifiers for the DefaultTableModel.
      • All subsequent rows are treated as data rows.
      If a row is physically missing within the data range (between first and last row numbers), an empty row (a Vector with no elements, though typically it should match column count) is added to the model. Cells within a row are read up to the last cell number of that row; missing cells (where row.getCell(c, Row.MissingCellPolicy.CREATE_NULL_AS_BLANK) returns a blank cell) are converted to their string representation (often an empty string). All cell values are converted to strings using Cell#toString() before being added to the model.

      Parameters:
      file - The Excel (XLSM) File to read. Must not be null and must exist.
      sheetIndex - The zero-based index of the sheet to read from the workbook.
      Returns:
      A DefaultTableModel populated with data from the specified sheet. Column headers are derived from the first row of the sheet.
      Throws:
      FileNotFoundException - if the specified file does not exist.
      org.apache.poi.openxml4j.exceptions.InvalidFormatException - if the file format is invalid for XSSFWorkbook (e.g., not OOXML based).
      IOException - if an I/O error occurs while reading the file or workbook.
      IllegalArgumentException - if sheetIndex is out of bounds.
      Exception - for other potential low-level errors during Apache POI processing.
